title: Melaleuca decora
intro: Melaleuca decora, also known as the ornamental melaleuca or snow-in-spring, is a species of shrub or small tree native to the eastern coast of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from colds, fever, and skin infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Melaleuca decora include cineole, a terpene with antiseptic and analgesic properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory infections and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Melaleuca decora is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The classification of Melaleuca decora places it within the genus Melaleuca, which includes over 200 species of paperbarks and honey-myrtles. The Myrtaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, often found in coastal and tropical regions. Further classification of Melaleuca decora places it in the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes several other families of flowering plants.

side_effects: Melaleuca decora is not well-documented, but based on the general information about Melaleuca species, consuming or using these plants can be associated with some side effects.
